author: NewsBreak Original
text:
By Samyarup Chowdhury ,
9 hours ago Donald Trump ‘s “controversial” Defense Secretary nominee Peter “Pete” Hegseth had reportedly made scathing remarks in the past about the President-elect that he “would not want to hear,” according to a CNN analyst.
Democratic CNN analyst Julie Roginsky pointed out during her recent TV appearance that a lot of people Trump has named as his cabinet nominations have a record of having “trashed” him sometime in the past, Knewz.com has learned.
Defense Secretary nominee Peter “Pete” Hegseth had reportedly made scathing remarks in the past about the President-elect. BY: @petehegseth/Instagram, MEGA Speaking on the show CNN Newsroom With Jim Acosta , Roginsky revealed that Hegseth – who has made headlines over his Defense Secretary nomination, his tattoos, and more – has previously said “ridiculous” things about Trump.
She said there is a long line of Trump critics who went on to “bend the knee” to the President-elect.
Notably, the list also includes Vice President-elect J.D. Vance —who not only was a Trump critic but also wrote an article heavily criticizing Republican policies back when he was a law student at Yale.
Notably, one of the major points of the essay Vance wrote at the time was how the Republicans “actively” alienated “every growing bloc of the American electorate—Blacks, Latinos, the youth,” adding that “you can’t depend solely on the single shrinking bloc of the electorate—Whites.”
Vice President-elect J.D. Vance is also a former Trump critic. BY: X/Zach D Roberts On the other hand, the 2024 Trump campaign saw Vance propagate an allegedly “hostile” anti-immigrant rhetoric—including fanning rumors of Haitian immigrants eating people’s pets in Ohio.
During her appearance on CNN Newsroom With Jim Acosta on Thursday, November 21, Roginsky listed Florida Senator Marco Rubio , National Intelligence Director nominee Tulsi Gabbard, and Fox News host and Defense Secretary nominee Hegseth.
“Pete Hegseth, who I used to work with at Fox privately would say ridiculous things about Donald Trump, things that Trump would not want to hear,” the CNN analyst said in conversation with Jim Acosta and Brad Todd.
“Marco Rubio, I’m old enough to remember, used to make fun of Donald Trump’s genitalia,” Roginsky added.
The CNN analyst claimed Hegseth has previously said “ridiculous” things about Trump. BY: MEGA Trump recently named Rubio as his choice for the United States Secretary of State, with reports suggesting that the decision might be based on the Florida Senator’s aggressively anti-China ideology.
Rubio has been a staunch supporter of Israel as well, showing an equally passionate “hardline” stance against Iran .
Rubio’s rumored Secretary of State nomination came amidst his statements suggesting the U.S. should turn its military aid away from Ukraine and rather focus it on Taiwan in a bid to help the island democracy fight Chinese oppression.
The CNN analyst further pointed out that “the majority of the Republicans who serve in the United States Senate now” have criticized Trump until they “all bent the knee.”
“They all knelt before Zod,” Roginsky remarked, seemingly comparing President-elect Trump to the Superman villain General Zod.
Jim Acosta, the host of the show, chimed in, saying, “I mean, Trump kind of collects past critics like Infinity Stones, if I could throw in a Avengers reference there.”
RFK Jr. has previously compared Trump Adolf Hitler and called his supporters “idiots and bootlickers.” BY: MEGA “I mean, that is something he’s done time and again. And it seems to me he seems to be doing it with a lot of the picks in his cabinet. Maybe even his vice president,” Acosta added.
However, Brad Todd seemed to view it as a sign that Trump has gotten to a point where “he can get over past criticism.”
“You know, that’s a thing he’s he’s grown toward. He doesn’t change much, but that’s one thing he’s changed,” Toss said on the show.
It is worth noting that Trump recently named former independent Presidential candidate Robert F. Kennedy Jr. his nominee for the position of Health Secretary, while the vocal anti-vaxxer has previously compared the President-elect to Adolf Hitler and called his supporters “idiots and bootlickers.”
